page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 24 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz, the server is configured with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1200 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this model.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong CPU performance. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both rank in the top 25% of tested instances, with scores of 2849.98 and 67555.75 respectively. PassMark CPU physics testing also yields a strong result of 7022.49. Other metrics fall within the average range, including a PassMark CPU score of 43837.49 and a memory score of 2750.59. Large-block memory bandwidth is measured at 141.36 GB/sec for read operations and 71.93 GB/sec for write operations. Database operations and Redis SET performance are average, with Redis achieving 42.05 million operations per second.

The shared CPU allocation presents a resource-sharing tradeoff, meaning performance may vary depending on host node neighbor activity, despite the strong raw benchmark results. However, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per core and the inclusion of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD make this instance a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, non-shared CPU execution. It is suitable for large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, data buffering, and general-purpose memory-heavy applications.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 24 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a bundled 1200 GB local NVMe SSD. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, benchmarks show strong CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core performance placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 42.05 million operations per second. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the instance highly suitable for memory-optimized workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that benefit from large memory capacities without requiring dedicated CPU cores.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance featuring 192 GB of RAM and local NVMe storage for high-capacity data buffering and caching workloads.
